2605.07273.title: From Clouds to Hallucinations: Atmospheric Retrieval Hijacking in Remote Sensing Vision-Language RAG
2605.07273.paper_summary: Multimodal RAG systems increasingly rely on vision-language retrievers to ground visual queries in external textual evidence. Existing adversarial studies on RAG mainly manipulate the retrieval corpus or memory, while attacks on vision-language and remote sensing models typically target end-task predictions. Input-space threats to the evidence retrieval stage of remote sensing multimodal RAG remain underexplored. To address this gap, we introduce CloudWeb, an atmospheric retrieval hijacking attack that modifies only the input image while keeping the retriever, generator, and knowledge base fixed at deployment. CloudWeb overlays parameterized cloud- and haze-like patterns on remote sensing images and optimizes them with a retrieval-oriented objective that pulls adversarial image embeddings toward target atmospheric evidence, suppresses source-scene evidence, enforces rank separation, and regularizes naturalness and coverage. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first study of retrieval-stage atmospheric evidence hijacking in remote sensing multimodal RAG. We evaluate CloudWeb on a seven-dataset remote sensing RAG benchmark with five CLIP-style retrievers, including GeoRSCLIP, RemoteCLIP, OpenAI CLIP, and OpenCLIP, together with downstream vision-language generators. Across retrievers, CloudWeb consistently outperforms clean retrieval, handcrafted atmospheric baselines, random cloud perturbations, and fixed variants in injecting weather-related evidence into top-ranked results. On GeoRSCLIP ViT-B/32, Weather@5 increases from 0.71\% to 43.29\%. Downstream generation further shows measurable weather hallucination and semantic shift, indicating that retrieval-stage hijacking can propagate to the final RAG response. These findings reveal a practical failure mode: natural-looking atmospheric changes can compromise evidence retrieval before generation begins.
2605.07273.paper_summary_zh: 多模態 RAG 系統越來越依賴視覺-語言檢索器，將視覺查詢與外部文本證據相結合。現有的對 RAG 的對抗性研究主要操控檢索語料庫或記憶體，而對於視覺-語言和遙感模型的攻擊通常針對最終任務預測。對於遙感多模態 RAG 的證據檢索階段的輸入空間威脅仍然未被充分探索。為了解決這一空白，我們引入了 CloudWeb，一種大氣檢索劫持攻擊，它僅修改輸入圖像，同時在部署時保持檢索器、生成器和知識庫的固定。CloudWeb 在遙感圖像上覆蓋參數化的雲和霧狀圖案，並以檢索導向的目標進行優化，將對抗性圖像嵌入拉向目標大氣證據，抑制源場景證據，強制排名分離，並正則化自然性和覆蓋率。據我們所知，這是首次研究遙感多模態 RAG 中檢索階段的大氣證據劫持。我們在七個數據集的遙感 RAG 基準上評估 CloudWeb，使用五個 CLIP 風格的檢索器，包括 GeoRSCLIP、RemoteCLIP、OpenAI CLIP 和 OpenCLIP，以及下游的視覺-語言生成器。在各檢索器中，CloudWeb 在將與天氣相關的證據注入排名最高的結果方面，始終超越乾淨檢索、手工製作的大氣基準、隨機雲擾動和固定變體。在 GeoRSCLIP ViT-B/32 上，Weather@5 從 0.71\% 增加到 43.29\%。下游生成進一步顯示可測量的天氣幻覺和語義轉移，表明檢索階段的劫持可以傳播到最終的 RAG 回應。這些發現揭示了一種實際的失效模式：自然看起來的大氣變化可以在生成開始之前妨礙證據檢索。
2605.07273.author: Jiaju Han et.al.
2605.07273.authors: Jiaju Han, Chao Li, Chengyin Hu, Qike Zhang, Xuemeng Sun, Xin Wang, Fengyu Zhang, Xiang Chen, Yiwei Wei, Jiahuan Long, Jiujiang Guo
